zh.lifeSummary: 當Heinrich Bach III.於大約1311年出生在德國貝也奧斯包格時，他的父親Heinrich Bach II.33歲，母親Gertrud30歲。他於1334年與Anna Langenmantel von Sparren在德國貝也奧斯包格奧斯包格結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他在德國貝也奧斯包格奧斯包格被列為geschlechter, ratsherr。他去世於1362年8月23日在他的家鄉享年52歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Heinrich Bach III.于大约1311年出生在德国貝也奧斯包格时，他的父亲Heinrich Bach II.33岁，母亲Gertrud30岁。他于1334年与Anna Langenmantel von Sparren在德国貝也奧斯包格奧斯包格结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他在德国貝也奧斯包格奧斯包格被列为geschlechter, ratsherr。他去世于1362年8月23日在他的家乡享年52岁。

nameMeanings.name: Bach
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Hans, Kurt, Fritz, Klaus, Horst, Johann, Alfons, Bernhard, Erna, Ernst.

German: topographic name for someone who lived by a stream, Middle High German bach ‘stream’. This surname is also found in some other European countries, most notably in Denmark and France (Alsace and Lorraine), but also in e.g. the Netherlands, Czechia (see also 5 below), and Croatia.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name from German Bach ‘stream, creek’.

variants.name: Bage
meanings: English (Durham and Yorkshire): possibly from Middle English bagge, Anglo-Norman French bage ‘badge, cognizance, emblem’, perhaps denoting someone who wore one as a sign of affiliation to a family or guild.
English: habitational name from The Bage in Dorstone, The Bage Farm in Madley, or Bage in Kington, all in Herefordshire. These are all names derived from Old English bæce ‘stream’; compare Bach 3.
French: variant of Bague, from the ancient Germanic personal name Bago, a short form of compound names based on the element bag ‘to fight’.

variants.name: Batch
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of   Bach   3.

variants.name: Baughan
meanings: Welsh: from the adjective bychan ‘little’, a diminutive of bach (see Bach 4), originally in general used to distinguish father and son bearing the same name in genealogies. This word usually appears in Anglicized surnames in the lenited form Vaughan .
History: A Welsh family of this name was established from an early date in Great Rollright, Oxfordshire, England. The name was probably brought to North America from Oxfordshire rather than Wales.

variants.name: Bartholomew
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Bertilmew, Bertelmy, a borrowing of the Old French form of the Biblical personal name Bartholomew (from Latin Bartholomaeus; Hebrew ‘son of Talmai’, said to mean ‘having many furrows’, i.e. rich in land). This was an extremely popular personal name in Christian Europe, with many vernacular derivatives. It derived its popularity from the apostle Saint Bartholomew (Matthew 10:3), who was, among other things, the patron saint of tanners, vintners, and butlers.
As an Irish name, it has been used as an Anglicized form of Mac Pharthaláin (see McFarlane ).

variants.name: Back
meanings: English (Devon, Kent, Sussex, and Norfolk): from the Middle English personal name Bakke (Old English Bacca). It is of uncertain origin, but may have been a byname in the same sense as 3.
English: nickname from Middle English bakke ‘bat’, of uncertain application, perhaps a nickname for a person with poor eyesight, from the expression ‘blind as a bat’.

English: from Middle English bakke ‘back’ (Old English bæc), hence a nickname for someone with a hunched back or some other noticeable peculiarity of the back or spine, or a topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or ridge, or at the rear of a settlement.

variants.name: Baugh
meanings: Welsh: probably an Anglicized form of Bach , from Welsh bach ‘little’.
Americanized form of German   Bach  .

variants.name: Baechle
meanings: South German (Bächle): Swabian-Alemannic variant of Bach , from a diminutive of Middle High German bach ‘stream’. Compare Bachle and Beckley .

variants.name: Bash
meanings: English: variant of   Bach   3.

Jewish: acronymic surname from the first letters of Hebrew ben ‘son of’ and of a personal name such as Shelomo (see Solomon ), Shemuel (see Samuel ), Shimon (see Simon ) or Shimshon (see Samson ); see also Brill . In North America, this surname is also an altered form of the variant Basch .
Americanized form of German   Basch  .

variants.name: Bache
meanings: German and English: variant of   Bach   1, 3.
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Bartholomaeus (see Bach 6, Bartholomew ).
East German: occupational name for a butcher from Middle High German bache ‘ham, smoked slab of bacon’.

variants.name: Bachofer
meanings: German: topographic name for a farmer living by a stream (see   Bach   and   Hofer  ).

nameMeanings.name: Heinrich
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from heim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power’. See Henry .

variants.name: Henry
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

en.lifeSummary: When Heinrich Bach III. was born about 1311, in Augsburg, Bavaria, Germany, his father, Heinrich Bach II., was 33 and his mother, Gertrud, was 30. He married Anna Langenmantel von Sparren in 1334, in Augsburg, Augsburg, Bavaria, Germany. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. His occupation is listed as geschlechter, ratsherr in Augsburg, Augsburg, Bavaria, Germany. He died on 23 August 1362, in his hometown, at the age of 52.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Heinrich Bach III. est né environ 1311, à Augsburg, Bayern, Allemagne, son père, Heinrich Bach II., avait 33 ans et sa mère, Gertrud, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Anna Langenmantel von Sparren en 1334, à Augsburg, Augsburg, Bayern, Allemagne. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. Sa profession de son indiquée est geschlechter, ratsherr à Augsburg, Augsburg, Bayern, Allemagne. Il est décédé le 23 août 1362, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 52 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Heinrich Bach III. um 1311 in Augsburg, Bayern, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Heinrich Bach II. 33 und seine Mutter Gertrud 30. Er hat 1334 in Augsburg, Augsburg, Bayern, Deutschland Anna Langenmantel von Sparren geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. sein Beruf ist als geschlechter, ratsherr in Augsburg, Augsburg, Bayern, Deutschland aufgeführt. Er starb am 23. August 1362 im Alter von 52 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Heinrich Bach III. è nato il circa 1311 nel Augsburg, Bayern, Germania, il suo padre, Heinrich Bach II., aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Gertrud, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Anna Langenmantel von Sparren nel 1334, nel Augsburg, Augsburg, Bayern, Germania. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie. La sua occupazione è indicata come geschlechter, ratsherr nel Augsburg, Augsburg, Bayern, Germania. Morì il 23 agosto 1362, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 52 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Heinrich Bach III.が約1311年にドイツバイエルンアウグスブルグで生まれた時，父Heinrich Bach II.は33歳，母Gertrudは30歳であった。1334年にドイツバイエルンアウグスブルグアウグスブルグでAnna Langenmantel von Sparrenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。ドイツバイエルンアウグスブルグアウグスブルグでの職業はgeschlechter, ratsherrと記録されている。彼は1362年8月23日に故郷で52歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Heinrich Bach III.(이)가 대략 1311년 독일 베이른 오그스버그에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Heinrich Bach II.(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Gertrud(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1334년 독일 베이른 오그스버그 오그스버그에서 Anna Langenmantel von Sparren(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그의 직업은 독일 베이른 오그스버그 오그스버그에서 geschlechter, ratsherr이었다. 그는 1362년 8월 23일 그의 고향에서 52세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Heinrich Bach III. nasceu aproximadamente 1311, em Augsburg, Bayern, Alemanha, seu pai, Heinrich Bach II., tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Gertrud, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Anna Langenmantel von Sparren em 1334, em Augsburg, Augsburg, Bayern, Alemanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas. Sua profissão aparece como geschlechter, ratsherr em Augsburg, Augsburg, Bayern, Alemanha. Ele faleceu em 23 de agosto de 1362, em sua cidade natal, com 52 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Heinrich Bach III. родился около 1311 г. в местности аугсбург, беиирн, Германия, его отцу по имени Heinrich Bach II. было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Gertrud, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Anna Langenmantel von Sparren в 1334 г. в местности аугсбург, аугсбург, беиирн, Германия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери.  Он работал по профессии geschlechter, ratsherr в местности аугсбург, аугсбург, беиирн, Германия. Он умер 23 августа 1362 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 52 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Heinrich Bach III. nació aproximadamente 1311, en Augsburg, Bayern, Alemania, su papá, Heinrich Bach II., tenía 33 años y su mamá, Gertrud, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Anna Langenmantel von Sparren en 1334, en Augsburg, Augsburg, Bayern, Alemania. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas. Su ocupación registrada era geschlechter, ratsherr en Augsburg, Augsburg, Bayern, Alemania. Murió el 23 de agosto de 1362, en su pueblo, a la edad de 52 años.
